skill.name: pexo-agent
skill.description: Creates and manages Pexo AI video projects from text, uploaded media, scripts, audio, or product references using shell helpers and the Pexo API.
skill.summary: Generate multi-shot AI videos through Pexo project, chat, upload, polling, and delivery workflows.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many hits, and most command-substitution and documentation examples are normal shell wrapper behavior rather than hidden malware. AI review confirms elevated risk because the skill includes prompt-priority language, sources a user config file as shell code, reads API credentials, transfers user prompts and media to a remote service, and downloads remote assets to local storage.

high_findings.title: Prompt Priority Override in Skill Instructions
high_findings.description: The skill declares a language rule as highest priority and non-negotiable. This is prompt-priority language inside untrusted skill content and can conflict with platform, system, or user instructions, even though it does not directly tell the reviewer to skip security checks.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain priority escalation language directed at the assistant. Confidence is high because this is semantic instruction hierarchy manipulation, although the content is not overtly malicious.

high_findings.title: Shell Sources User-Writable Config File
high_findings.description: The shared script sources the Pexo config file from the user home directory or PEXO_CONFIG path. A shell source operation executes any commands in that file, so a malicious or tampered config path could run arbitrary shell code when any Pexo script starts.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source calls are explicit and occur before API operations. Confidence is very high for the execution behavior, while exploitability depends on control of the config file or PEXO_CONFIG path.

medium_findings.title: External API Receives Prompts, Credentials, and Project Data
medium_findings.description: The scripts send Authorization headers and user-provided message bodies to PEXO_BASE_URL. This is expected for a Pexo integration, but it means prompts, project identifiers, and account credentials are exposed to the configured remote service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network and credential flow is clear in the shell code. It is a legitimate integration pattern, so the risk is medium rather than a confirmed malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Media Upload to Remote Storage
medium_findings.description: The upload helper accepts a local file path, obtains a remote upload credential, and transfers the file with curl. This is core functionality, but users may accidentally send sensitive images, video, or audio to external storage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly uploads local files and the documentation instructs agents to upload user-provided media. Confidence is high that external transfer occurs, but it is disclosed as intended behavior.

medium_findings.title: Remote Asset Download to Local Cache
medium_findings.description: The asset fetch helper downloads signed URLs into the Pexo temp directory. This can write remote content to disk and should be treated as untrusted downloaded media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The download and local write behavior is explicit. Risk depends on how callers open or process downloaded media after retrieval.

low_findings.title: Scanner False Positives From Documentation and Normal Shell Parsing
low_findings.description: Many static hits are markdown command examples, ordinary jq and curl usage, or command substitutions used to capture output. The Windows SAM database alert is a false positive caused by the word SAME in the language rule, not evidence of Windows credential access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found benign context for these broad scanner patterns. Confidence is high because the cited examples are documentation, parsing, or false keyword matches rather than hidden credential theft.

low_findings.title: Diagnostic System Reconnaissance Is Disclosed
low_findings.description: The doctor script checks installed commands, network reachability, and API key validity. These checks are disclosed diagnostics and do not enumerate unrelated files or secrets beyond the required Pexo configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior matches the stated diagnostic purpose. It still has privacy relevance because it reports environment details and contacts the configured service.

dangerous_patterns.title: source on Configurable Path
dangerous_patterns.description: Using source on PEXO_CONFIG or $HOME/.pexo/config is dangerous if the file can contain anything except simple assignments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is a direct shell execution primitive. The pattern is confirmed in two code paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent to Configured Base URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The authorization header is built from PEXO_API_KEY and attached to curl requests to PEXO_BASE_URL.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential transport is explicit and expected for API access. It becomes dangerous if PEXO_BASE_URL is misconfigured or malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: User File Upload Through Signed URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The upload helper transfers local file contents to a server-provided upload URL using curl.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The upload flow is direct and confirmed. Risk comes from accidental upload of sensitive media or trust in the returned upload URL.

content.value_statement: Video generation often requires model choices, prompts, uploads, and status polling. This skill wraps Pexo workflows so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can create and deliver finished videos.
actual_capabilities: Creates Pexo video projects and returns project identifiers.
Uploads supported image, video, and audio files to Pexo projects.
Sends user messages and asset references to the Pexo chat API.
Polls project state and maps backend status to next actions.
Fetches final or preview asset details and downloads available media files.
Runs diagnostics for config, dependencies, network access, and API key validity.
limitations: Requires a Pexo account, API key, curl, jq, and file command support.
Sends prompts, project data, and uploaded media to the Pexo service.
Generated videos depend on account credits, backend availability, and model behavior.
Does not provide local-only video generation or offline rendering.

use_cases.title: Produce Product Ads
use_cases.description: Create short product videos from a product image, URL, or brief for ads and social channels.

use_cases.title: Create Social Clips
use_cases.description: Turn a short idea or script into vertical videos for TikTok, Reels, or YouTube Shorts.

use_cases.title: Prototype Brand Concepts
use_cases.description: Generate multi-shot video drafts with music, subtitles, and revisions before a production review.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Video Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with no uploaded assets.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15 second video about [topic] for [audience]. Use a [tone] style and make it suitable for [platform].

prompt_templates.title: Product Video From Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a local product image as the main reference.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product video for this item. Highlight [benefit one], [benefit two], and [call to action]. Use the uploaded image as the reference.

prompt_templates.title: Scripted Explainer
prompt_templates.scenario: Provide structured narration and visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make an explainer video from this script: [script]. Use clear narration, readable subtitles, and three visual scenes.

prompt_templates.title: Campaign Variant Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for multiple creative directions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three video concepts for [campaign]. Keep the offer consistent, vary the visual style, and ask me to choose a preview before final delivery.

output_examples.input: Create a short product ad from a shoe photo.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a Pexo project, uploads the photo, submits the brief with an image asset tag, and returns a project link for tracking.

output_examples.input: Check whether my Pexo setup works.
output_examples.output: The skill runs diagnostics and reports config status, dependency checks, network reachability, and API key validation without showing the full key.

output_examples.input: Show me the finished video for this project.
output_examples.output: The skill fetches the final asset, downloads it to the local Pexo temp folder when available, and provides the signed download URL.

best_practices: Confirm the user wants to send prompts and media to Pexo before uploading sensitive files.
Use the diagnostic script before starting production to catch config and credit problems early.
Poll at the documented interval and avoid duplicate chat messages while generation is running.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential media unless the user has approved external processing.
Do not paste or display full API keys in chat, logs, or support messages.
Do not retry credit failures repeatedly before the user has added credits or changed the request.

faq.question: Does this skill generate videos locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the Pexo API and requires network access to Pexo services.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: It requires PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL in the environment or a Pexo config file.

faq.question: Can it use uploaded media?
faq.answer: Yes. It can upload supported image, video, and audio files to a Pexo project.

faq.question: Where are downloaded assets stored?
faq.answer: Downloaded assets are cached in the Pexo temp directory, or in PEXO_TMP_DIR when configured.

faq.question: Why does generation take time?
faq.answer: Pexo performs remote video production and the skill polls until the backend reports the next action.

faq.question: Is this suitable for sensitive content?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts, media, project metadata, and generated assets are processed by an external service.
